prompt:
The React export panel is showing Export enabled for users who have either a paid workspace or the export permission, even though production policy requires both. The API response itself says allowed, so this is not a button-only rendering bug. This repository has a UI view-model seam, an offline decision cache, a typed API adapter, and the server policy module; please follow the decision end to end and repair the source of authority.

Do not delete or bypass the client cache. Field crews deliberately reopen the last server decision while offline, including a previous denial, and an online render must refresh that cached value. Workspace lifecycle and region checks, denial reasons, the generated wire contract, cache isolation, malformed-response handling, and client-assumption resistance are all existing behavior.

Run `node --test test_policy_source.ts`. The test and shared generated contract are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: trace a feature decision across UI, offline cache, API adapter, and server modules to repair the server-owned policy
full_distill.baseline_evidence: node --test test_policy_source.ts fails because an active paid workspace without the export permission receives allowed=true instead of permission_missing
full_distill.success_contract: the server requires active lifecycle, eligible plan, matching region, and explicit permission while the client continues to cache online decisions and replay the exact last decision offline
adversarial_factors: direct server-policy tests reject a UI-only or adapter-only override
a React component contract consumes the controller decision and preserves disabled, reason, source, label, and callback behavior without re-owning policy
offline tests use a transport that throws if called and require cached allow and deny decisions to remain usable
an online denial must overwrite a stale cached allow, rejecting put-if-absent caches and allow-only persistence
clientAssumedAllowed is deliberately true in denied cases and must not bypass server state
cache keys vary by workspace, user, and region, preventing a hard-coded or cross-user answer
